name: Catholic Church of St Paul of the Cross and the Immaculate Conception
description:
The Church of the Immaculate Conception and St Paul of the Cross is a welcoming Catholic parish located on New Canterbury Road in Dulwich Hill. Serving the local Dulwich Hill area and the broader multicultural community, this vibrant parish specialises in offering pastoral care and holding services in both English and Filipino. It is a favourite gathering centre for the local Filipino-Australian community, hosting a regular monthly Filipino Mass at 11:30 am on the first Sunday of each month.

Parishioners and visitors highly appreciate the warm, accommodating nature of the community, which includes a dedicated play space for children. The parish team, led by Dean Louis, is widely recognised for being friendly and supportive. Architecturally, the church features an imposing dark brick facade that opens into a light and airy interior, boasting excellent acoustics and a loft area designed for live streaming services. Outside, a lovely devotional garden provides a peaceful sanctuary for quiet reflection. Whether attending Mass, participating in community events, or celebrating a wedding, visitors can expect a serene and supportive environment in the heart of Sydney.

description: St. Martha's Catholic Church in Strathfield, NSW is a welcoming Catholic parish community. The parish serves the local community and hosts a dedicated Filipino (Tagalog) Mass on the 4th Sunday of the month at 11:00 am, fostering spiritual growth and community connection.

name: St. Michael's Catholic Church
description: St. Michael's Catholic Church in Hurstville features an active Filipino community Cenacle group that coordinates devotions and community hospitality.

name: St. Michael's Catholic Parish, Lane Cove
description: St. Michael's Catholic Parish in Lane Cove hosts a monthly Filipino Mass on the 4th Sunday of every month at 11:00 am, offering religious services and community fellowship for the Filipino-Australian community.

name: St. Patrick's Catholic Church, Mortlake
description: St. Patrick's Catholic Parish in Mortlake hosts a dedicated monthly Filipino Mass on the 4th Sunday of the month at 11:00 am, serving the local Filipino-Australian community.

name: Staffhouse Australia
description: Staffhouse Australia is an affiliate office of Staffhouse International Resources, a Department of Migrant Workers (DMW) licensed recruitment agency specializing in placing skilled Filipino workers with Australian employers.

name: Starlight Bakery
description: Established in 2000, we built Starlight Bakery to bring the most authentic Filipino breads in Sydney. Today we continue to build on our history and culture, baking the same authentic family recipe and taste of home here abroad.

name: Sto. Nino Oriental Mini Mart
description: Filipino and Asian grocery store in St Marys, Sydney, offering imported groceries, spices, specialty sauces, and frozen goods.

name: Stojanovic & Associates
description: Stojanovic & Associates is a migration agency specializing in partner visas for Australian & Filipino couples, led by Danijela Stojanovic (MARN: 0958278) with over 15 years of experience.

name: Suki Kart
description: Suki Kart is a Filipino grocery store in Hornsby, Sydney. It offers a wide range of authentic Filipino products including frozen and dry goods, breads, cakes, desserts, and other Pinoy delicacies, as well as money remittance and Balikbayan box services.

name: Suki Kart & Linalyn's Rooty Hill
description: Suki Kart & Linalyn's Rooty Hill is a well-regarded Filipino and Asian grocery store located on Rooty Hill Road South, right near the heart of Rooty Hill. The store offers a wide variety of quality Filipino pantry staples, fresh produce, frozen items, and snacks. Customers highly praise the warm and welcoming customer service from the friendly staff.

name: Summer Vibe
description: A verified Filipino cafe in Sydney.

name: Sunny Corner Cafe
description: Formerly known as Eskinita Café, Sunny Corner Cafe is a small, cozy Filipino breakfast spot located right across from Doonside Station. They serve generous portions of mouthwatering dishes such as sisig, tapsilog, and pancit, paired with Basque cheesecake, coffee, and halo-halo.

name: Super Pinoy Sari-Sari Store
description: Super Pinoy Sari-Sari Store is a local Filipino grocery and convenience store located on Queen Street in St Marys, Sydney. The store offers a wide selection of authentic Pinoy foods, snacks, pantry items, and fresh delicacies. Customers appreciate the friendly, kind, and accommodating service from the owners.

name: Superior Bakeshop & More
description: A well-known family-run Filipino bakery in Mount Druitt serving a wide range of authentic Filipino-style baked goods, traditional breads, pastries, and sweet snacks.

name: Supersave Convenience Store
description: A Filipino convenience store located in Berala, Sydney, offering a variety of Filipino snacks, groceries, and fresh food.

name: Sydney Australian-Filipino Seniors Inc. (SAFSI)
description: A registered charity and community organisation that organises social activities, cultural awareness programs, and support services for Filipino-Australian seniors in the Blacktown and Western Sydney area.

name: Sydney Cebu Lechon
description: Filipino restaurant in Newtown, Sydney. Famous for charcoal-roasted pork belly (lechon) and authentic Cebuano-style dishes. Specialises in traditional Cebu lechon preparation.

name: Sydney Eco Cleaning
description: Sydney Eco Cleaning is an experienced team of professional Filipino cleaners offering eco-friendly, non-toxic, and biodegradable commercial, strata, office, and residential cleaning services across Sydney.

name: Sydney Elshaddai Church
description: Sydney chapter of El Shaddai DWXI PPFI Australia, a vibrant Filipino Catholic Charismatic renewal movement holding regular prayer fellowships and worship services at the Senior Citizen Centre in Blacktown.

name: Sydney FilOz Badminton
description: Sydney FilOz Badminton (SFB) is a vibrant community-based social sports group established in 2017 in Sydney, New South Wales. Originally founded by four Filipino expats seeking a recreational hobby, the association has grown significantly to over 1,000 members on social media. The group's primary mission is to foster strong community bonds, promote active and healthy lifestyles, and support new Filipino arrivals adapting to life in Australia through the shared love of badminton. They host weekly social badminton sessions welcoming players of all skill levels, from beginners to advanced. These regular games are held every Saturday from 1:00 PM to 4:00 PM at the Alpha Badminton Centre (Egerton) in Silverwater. SFB coordinates their session schedule, courts, and event queuing dynamically using the DoublesTeam app to ensure a seamless experience for all participants.

name: Sydney Filipino Social Club
description: The Sydney Filipino Social Club is a community and social group on Meetup for Filipinos and friends in Sydney. The group organizes regular activities such as dining out, drinks nights, karaoke, picnics, hikes, road trips, and sports (with basketball being a favorite) to strengthen the Filipino community.

name: Sydney Jokers Social Golf Club
description: Sydney Jokers Social Golf Club is a Filipino-Australian social organization in New South Wales, hosting weekly golf games, tournaments, and social events for members and guests.

name: Sydney Kali Academy
description: The Sydney Kali Academy is one of Australia's premier academies for Bruce Lee's Jeet Kune Do and Filipino Martial Arts (Kali). Teaching out of the Hurstville area of Sydney since 2003 in a relaxed, welcoming environment.

name: Sydney Lawyers and Associates
description: Sydney Lawyers and Associates Pty Ltd is a professional law firm in Parramatta, Sydney, offering legal services in Property Law and Conveyancing, Family Law, Business and Commercial Law, Criminal Law, Debt Recovery, Employment Law, Insurance Law, Litigation, and Wills/Probate. They have a Tagalog-speaking Filipino lawyer on staff.

name: TERESA'S Siopao & Catering
description: Family-owned Filipino takeaway and catering business in Doonside, NSW. Highly regarded in the local community for traditional Filipino steamed buns (siopao) and custom home-style party catering.

name: TFC Traditional Filipino Catering
description: TFC Traditional Filipino Catering is a family-owned business specializing in authentic Kapampangan Filipino cuisine, catering, and event food trays. They offer a wide range of hot Filipino foods, groceries, and snacks.

name: TNB Cafe
description: The Naked Barista Cafe serves up brunch and specialty brews in Rooty Hill with community at the heart. Features a Filipino-inspired menu including a longganisa brekky burger, chicken sisig pasta, and specialty lattes.

name: Tagalog Association of Australia Inc.
description: Tagalog Association of Australia Inc. (TAA) is a community organization in Sydney, New South Wales, established in 2012 to promote Tagalog heritage, Filipino language and cultural values through festivals, performances, and community discussions.

name: Taguan Cafe
description: Taguan Cafe is a lovely Filipino-themed café in Darlington, Sydney. Known for its delicious coffee and welcoming vibe, it serves comforting Filipino favorites like tapsilog, Tatay's adobo, traditional egg pies, and maja blanca.

name: Takam
description: Elevated, progressive modern Filipino restaurant in Darlinghurst, Sydney. Founded by chefs Francis Dela Cruz, Aileen Aguirre, and Lesley Roque, Takam is dedicated to a progressive take on classic Filipino dishes, blending traditional techniques and nostalgic flavours with native Australian ingredients like lemon myrtle, pepperberry, and salt bush.

name: Takam, Filipino Food Forward
description: Takam, Filipino Food Forward is a high-end, innovative Filipino restaurant in Darlinghurst, Sydney. They are known for serving premium, creative takes on traditional Filipino dishes, such as lechon, in a modern dining setting.

name: Takkam Cafe
description: Takkam Cafe was a Filipino café located in Bondi Junction, Sydney, NSW.

name: Tambayan Pinoy Variety Store
description: Tambayan Pinoy Variety Store is a Filipino grocery and variety shop located in Belmore, Sydney, offering a wide selection of imported Filipino goods and community products.

name: Tapsi Supermarket
description: Tapsi Supermarket is a Filipino grocery store located in HomeCo. Prestons, New South Wales. It offers a variety of Filipino products, ingredients, and daily fresh produce for the local community.

name: Tarro Foods
description: Tarro Foods is a popular, family-owned Filipino food truck located at the Liberty Service Station in Doonside, Sydney. It specializes in authentic Filipino street food and comfort delicacies, including highly-rated pork and chicken empanadas, chicken inasal, arroz caldo lugaw, beef pares, crispy pata, and gulaman.

name: Tata Rods
description: A Sydney-based food stall showcasing authentic Filipino cuisine, specializing in pork belly and sausages seasoned with traditional Philippine spices and ingredients.

name: Tatak Pinoy Land Down Under
description: Filipino and Asian grocery store in Blacktown, stocking a range of imported goods, food items, and products from the Philippines.

name: The 3 Bears Bakeshop
description: A home-based bakeshop in Baulkham Hills specializing in authentic Filipino cakes and pastries. Known for Ube cake, mocha cake, ensaymada, sylvanas, and sans rival, frequently appearing at pop-ups in Westpoint Blacktown.

name: The Bangketa Grocer
description: Filipino grocery shop in Penrith, providing authentic Filipino goods, pantry items, and imported products.

name: The Brown Beat Band
description: The Brown Beat Band is a Filipino-Australian live music group and cover band based in Sydney, NSW. They perform live music, pop, beat, and rock covers at local venues, community gatherings, and private events across the Sydney region.

name: The Filipino Saturday School
description: A volunteer-run Filipino community language school in Blacktown, Western Sydney, dedicated to teaching the younger generation and adult learners the Filipino language (Tagalog), history, traditions, and culture. Offers Basic Tagalog classes for primary and high school students (Year 1–12) and adults, as well as Bandurria music lessons. Founded and organised by community volunteers including coordinator Mr. Jade Cadelina.

name: The Filoz Restaurant
description: Popular Filipino restaurant previously located in Campbelltown, Sydney, known for authentic Filipino adobo, halo-halo, and a friendly community atmosphere. Currently relocating.

name: The Good Filo
description: A delightful Filipino dessert and cake shop in Sydney.

name: The Gospel Church of Jesus
description: A vibrant Filipino fellowship and Christian church group in Sydney.

name: The Handsome Mechanic (Andre Automotive PL)
description: Andre Automotive (trading as The Handsome Mechanic) is a highly-rated Filipino-Australian family-owned mechanical workshop based in Mount Druitt, Western Sydney. They provide honest car care advice, logbook servicing, pink and blue slip inspections, tyre services, and brake repairs, and are active sponsors of local Filipino community festivals.

name: The Kamayan
description: Filipino restaurant located in 55 Rooty Hill Rd N, Rooty Hill NSW 2766.

name: The Love of Jesus Christian Ministries
description: A Bible-based, Christ-centred community church in Glendenning, Western Sydney, led by Pastor Orlando Borja, with strong roots in the Filipino-Australian community and active mission work in the Philippines.

name: The Word Christian Mission
description: A non-denominational, bible-based Christian church and community founded by Filipino migrants in St Marys.

name: Tindahan Filipino-Asian Grocery
description: A verified Filipino shop in Sydney.

name: Tindahan Ni Juan
description: A premium Filipino sari-sari store and grocery shop in Sydney, stocking favorite Filipino products, snacks, and imported brands.

name: Tindahan ni Marekoy
description: An online Filipino grocery store delivering authentic Filipino products, snacks, and grocery essentials across Sydney and Australia.

name: Tita Carinderia
description: Authentic home-style Filipino restaurant in Marrickville, Sydney. Known for traditional Filipino comfort food and casual dining. Popular among the Filipino community for carinderia-style cooking.

name: Tita's Cakes
description: Tita's Cakes is a highly popular Filipino cake shop in Rooty Hill, near Blacktown, Sydney. They specialize in a contemporary take on Filipino desserts, including custom cakes, cupcakes, ube ensaymada, and other traditional Filipino sweet classics.

name: Tong Li Supermarket Cabramatta
description: A verified Filipino cafe in Sydney.

name: Tourism Philippines - Australia & New Zealand
description: The official tourism promotion agency of the Philippine Department of Tourism for the Australia and New Zealand region, promoting travel and cultural experiences in the Philippines.

name: Trixie Freed Weddings
description: Trixie Freed Weddings is a Sydney Wedding Planner and Celebrant, specialising in personalised weddings that reflect each couple’s personalities and unique love story. With a passion for event planning, extensive project management experience, and attention to detail, Trixie ensures every wedding is memorable.

name: UNSW Filipino Student Society (FiloSoc)
description: Student association at the University of New South Wales (UNSW) hosting social, cultural, and food events to foster Filipino community spirit among students.

name: UTS Filipino Student Association
description: The UTS Filipino Student Association (FSA UTS / FiloSoc) is a student-led community organization registered with ActivateUTS. The society aims to foster a support network for Filipino students and promote Filipino culture and heritage through social, cultural, and community-building events including boodle fights, vivid boat parties, beach walks, and karaoke sessions.

name: United Architects of the Philippines - Australia Chapter
description: A professional organization for Filipino architects residing in Australia and New Zealand. Chartered on March 6, 2021, at the Philippine Embassy in Canberra, it serves to unite Filipino architects, provide professional development, and promote collaboration within the built environment.

name: University of the Philippines Alumni Association of Australia - NSW Chapter
description: The University of the Philippines Alumni Association of Australia - NSW Chapter (UPAAA-NSW) is a community organization in Sydney, New South Wales, established to foster fellowship among UP alumni, support the community, and promote heritage and culture.

name: University of the Philippines Alumni Association of Australia - New South Wales
description: The University of the Philippines Alumni Association of Australia - New South Wales Chapter, Inc. (UPAAA-NSW) is a community organization for alumni of the University of the Philippines living in New South Wales. The association fosters community networking, supports student welfare, raises scholarship funds, and organizes cultural and civic activities in Sydney.

name: VUNO Physiotherapy & Rehabilitation
description: Physiotherapy clinic offering clinic-based, mobile and home-based physiotherapy services, and NDIS therapeutic support in Tagalog (Filipino) and English.

name: Valencia's Kitchenette
description: Authentic Filipino catering services in Sydney, specializing in quality traditional Filipino dishes and cuisines suitable for all occasions.

name: Victory Life Christian Church
description: Victory Life Christian Church is a physical church located in Blacktown, Western Sydney. Affiliated with Foursquare Church Australia, it serves the local community through weekly Sunday worship services, prayer, and ministries. It hosts a dedicated Filipino community service and fellowship on the first Sunday of every month.

name: Visa Hub Tech
description: Visa Hub Tech is a trusted Australian education and migration agency operated by registered migration agent Aubrey Pia Estacio (MARN: 2418377). The agency specializes in skilled migration, student visas, partner visas, and family visa pathways for the Filipino community.

name: Visayab Migration Services
description: Visayab Migration Services, a Filipino-owned migration agency in Sydney. The name derives from Visaya (Visayan), referring to one of the three major island groups in the Philippines.

name: Visayan Association of Australia Inc.
description: Visayan Association of Australia (VAA) Inc. is a community organization based in Liverpool, NSW, dedicated to promoting Filipino-Australian culture, language, and supporting charitable projects in the Visayas region of the Philippines.

name: West Sydney Community Church
description: A Filipino-connected Christian congregation in Glendenning, Western Sydney, led by Pastor Rey Luciano and Pastor Arnold "Dong" De Leon.

name: Wow Filipino Food
description: Wow Filipino Food is a popular Sydney eatery serving authentic, hawker-style Filipino street food. Best known for their signature Pork Sisig, grilled pork belly, and sweet-and-tangy barbecue skewers, they operate a weekend food stall at the Burwood Chinatown Night Market and Eastwood. They are operated by Mae and Richard Rodillas.

name: Woy woy Sari sari Filipino store
description: A verified Filipino shop in Sydney.

name: Yayin Serrano Hair and MakeUp Studio
description: Yayin Serrano Hair and MakeUp Studio is a professional Filipino-owned hair and beauty studio located in Hornsby, Sydney, NSW. They specialize in personalized hair treatments (including Nano Plasty, Keratin, and Brazilian Botox), custom coloring, formal makeup artistry, and modern haircuts.

name: Yummylicious Cafe
description: Yummylicious Cafe is a popular Filipino-influenced café in Campbelltown, Sydney, serving delicious all-day breakfast options including Tapsilog, Tocilog, Porksilog, Bangsilog, and Cornsilog alongside classic café items.

name: Zarate Mechanics
description: Zarate Mechanics is a family owned and operated business in Arndell Park. Our professional team provides comprehensive automotive services including log book servicing (including novated leases), wheel balancing and alignment, pink and blue slip inspections, brakes, suspension, exhaust repairs, and cutting-edge diagnostic testing.

name: bypau
description: 🎂 Classic Filipino Cakes and Customised Buttercream Cakes based in Granville, Sydney. Specializing in traditional sweets like Sansrival, Yema, Polvoron, Ube Macapuno Chiffon Cake, Ube Leche Flan Cake, Mango Bravo, and Brazo de Mercedes. Pre-orders only, pickup available at Granville.

name: mydentist Fairfield
description: mydentist is a family dentist in Fairfield owned and operated by Dr. Dennis Francisco and Dr. Victoria Francisco. We offer treatments such as cleaning, tooth extraction, crowns and bridges, denture fittings and at-home teeth whitening.
